skill.name: firecrawl-scrape
skill.description: Extract clean, LLM-ready Markdown and related page data from one or more URLs with Firecrawl.
skill.summary: Scrape URLs into clean Markdown, links, HTML, screenshots, or targeted page answers.
skill.author: firecrawl

security_audit.summary: The static findings are mostly false positives from Markdown code fences, code spans, example URLs, and relative documentation links. The real risk is operational: this skill authorizes a networked Firecrawl CLI to fetch user-provided URLs and optionally write output files, so users should review URLs and output paths before running it.

medium_findings.title: Network-Enabled External Command Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill permits Bash execution of firecrawl and npx firecrawl commands. This is expected for a scraping skill, but it can fetch arbitrary user-provided URLs through an external service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools block and command examples directly authorize external CLI execution. The purpose is legitimate, but the commands perform network requests against user-provided URLs.

medium_findings.title: User-Controlled Output Path
medium_findings.description: The command examples and option table allow an output path with -o or --output. The examples use .firecrawl paths, but a careless path could overwrite local files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file output option is clearly documented, but the examples steer users toward a local .firecrawl directory. The risk depends on the path chosen at runtime.

medium_findings.title: Remote Query Mode May Send Page Questions Externally
medium_findings.description: The --query option asks a question about page content and costs extra Firecrawl credits. This may send the prompt and retrieved page content to the service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The query feature is explicitly documented, but SKILL.md does not describe its transport or data handling details. The privacy concern is plausible because the skill uses a remote scraping CLI.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Ruby Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The reported backtick findings occur inside Markdown code fences or inline command references. They document CLI usage and are not Ruby code execution in this skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is Markdown, and the matched backticks delimit examples or option names. No Ruby source file or executable Ruby expression is present.

low_findings.title: Example URLs Are Benign Documentation
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL findings use example.com in usage examples. They do not indicate credential exfiltration, tracking, or a suspicious destination.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: example.com is a reserved documentation domain and appears only in command examples. No hidden network endpoint or encoded destination is present.

low_findings.title: Relative Links Misclassified as Path Traversal
low_findings.description: The ../ paths are Markdown links to sibling skill documentation. They are not file reads, writes, or runtime path traversal operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The relative paths are link targets in the See also section. They do not execute code or access files at runtime.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography locations are front matter text and a Markdown table header. No cryptographic algorithm or hashing operation appears in the file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain description text and option table formatting. I found no evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, or another weak cryptographic operation.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Scraping CLI
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill relies on firecrawl commands that make network requests for user-supplied URLs. This is necessary for the feature but should be treated as a network boundary crossing.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The tool allowlist and examples both show Firecrawl CLI execution. The network behavior is central to scraping URLs.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local File Output Option
dangerous_patterns.description: The documented output option writes scrape results to a path selected in the command. Users should prefer the documented .firecrawl directory.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The output behavior is explicitly documented. The security impact depends on whether the runtime path is constrained by the user.

content.user_title: Scrape Web Pages into Clean Markdown
content.value_statement: Web pages are often hard to reuse because they include scripts, navigation, and layout noise. This skill uses Firecrawl to extract clean page content for research, coding, and analysis workflows.
actual_capabilities: Scrapes one or more URLs with the Firecrawl CLI.
Extracts clean Markdown from static pages and JavaScript-rendered pages.
Can save Markdown or multi-format output to local files.
Supports main-content extraction to reduce navigation and footer noise.
Can return links, HTML, raw HTML, screenshots, or JSON when requested.
Can ask a targeted question about a page with query mode.
limitations: Requires the firecrawl or npx firecrawl command to be available.
Only works for pages that Firecrawl can access and render.
Query mode may cost extra Firecrawl credits.
Saved output paths should be reviewed before command execution.

use_cases.title: Research a Source Page
use_cases.description: Extract a clean version of an article, documentation page, or product page before summarizing it.

use_cases.title: Prepare Web Content for Coding Tasks
use_cases.description: Pull API documentation or framework pages into Markdown so an assistant can reason over the content.

use_cases.title: Compare Multiple Pages
use_cases.description: Scrape several related URLs concurrently and review the generated Markdown files together.

prompt_templates.title: Scrape One Page
prompt_templates.prompt: Scrape this URL into clean Markdown and save it under .firecrawl: https://example.com/page

prompt_templates.title: Extract Main Content
prompt_templates.prompt: Scrape this article with main content only, then summarize the key points: https://example.com/article

prompt_templates.title: Scrape Multiple URLs
prompt_templates.prompt: Scrape these three URLs concurrently and save each result in .firecrawl with clear names: https://example.com/a https://example.com/b https://example.com/c

prompt_templates.title: Render and Query a Page
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for JavaScript-rendered content and a targeted answer.
prompt_templates.prompt: Wait for JavaScript rendering, scrape this pricing page, and answer which plan includes enterprise support: https://example.com/pricing

output_examples.input: Scrape a documentation page and save it as Markdown.
output_examples.output: A local Markdown file is created with the main headings, page text, and useful links from the source page.

output_examples.input: Scrape a JavaScript-rendered product page after a short wait.
output_examples.output: The result includes rendered page content instead of only the initial empty shell.

output_examples.input: Ask a focused question about a pricing page.
output_examples.output: The assistant returns a concise answer based on the scraped page content and can cite the saved source text.

best_practices: Quote URLs so shell characters are handled correctly.
Save outputs under .firecrawl to keep scraped files organized.
Prefer plain scraping before using query mode for deeper review.
anti_patterns: Do not scrape private or sensitive URLs without authorization.
Do not write output to broad or important project paths.
Do not rely on query mode when you need the full source content.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It uses Firecrawl to scrape URLs and return clean Markdown or other requested page formats.

faq.question: Can it handle JavaScript-rendered pages?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill documents a wait option for pages that need time to render.

faq.question: Can it scrape more than one URL?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill supports multiple URLs and notes that they are scraped concurrently.

faq.question: Where should output files go?
faq.answer: The documented convention is to save scrape outputs under the .firecrawl directory.

faq.question: Is query mode always recommended?
faq.answer: No. The skill recommends plain scraping first and using query mode only for targeted answers.

faq.question: What security review result applies?
faq.answer: The skill has medium operational risk because it runs a networked CLI and can write local output files.
